scope_of_revision: Item A ONLY. Items F, C, B and the F->C->B->A ordering rationale are UNCHANGED and approved as-is per QA pass1.

qa_findings_addressed.qa_finding: Class-1 classification criterion is wrong for most listed git rules (reset --hard, push --force/--delete, checkout -- ., restore --source, revert <hash> are argument-signal, not verb-signal).
qa_findings_addressed.resolution: FIXED. The class boundary is redrawn on match-key LOCATION against the verified stripper semantics (bash_context_strip.py:305-330): a rule is convertible ONLY if its match result is identical raw-vs-stripped. All five named git rules (and the rest: update-ref/branch -D :1421, cherry-pick/rebase :1441, stash push/save :1350-1361) are moved to the explicit DO-NOT-CONVERT set because their danger token is a quotable ARGUMENT of the non-DANGER command word 'git'.

qa_findings_addressed.qa_finding: Concrete false-negative introduced: git reset "--hard" executes a real hard reset but strips to 'git reset ', silently disabling the block.
qa_findings_addressed.resolution: FIXED + DOCUMENTED. The quoted-argument false-negative mechanism is now the lead Edge Case in the ticket with a worked before/after stripped-view proof (RAW 'git reset "--hard"' matches and blocks; STRIPPED 'git reset ' does not match -> the introduced bug). The reset rule is no longer a conversion target.

qa_findings_addressed.qa_finding: Prescribed DANGER_COMMANDS remediation ('add the command word, i.e. git') is self-defeating: it un-strips ALL git args globally, re-introducing the quoted false positives and perturbing the shipped rm/mv interaction.
qa_findings_addressed.resolution: FIXED. The 'add to DANGER_COMMANDS' step is explicitly RETRACTED in both ticket and context. Net DANGER_COMMANDS change this cycle is NONE. The two convertible rules need no entry: dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred is command-word-anchored (nothing to expose), and kill is already a DANGER command. bash_context_strip.py:26 stays {killall,pkill,kill,rm,mv}; the file edit is comment-only (:5).

qa_findings_addressed.qa_finding: Conflation/omission of the second kill rule at :1311 (kill <PID>, argument-signal) from the Class-1/Class-2 sweep.
qa_findings_addressed.resolution: FIXED. :1311 is ruled on explicitly and placed IN the convertible set, with the principled reason: it is argument-anchored but kill is already in DANGER_COMMANDS, so the stripper UNQUOTES its args (kill "1234" -> kill 1234) and the match is identical raw-vs-stripped. This mirrors the already-stripped kill -sig rule at :1119. It is one of the EXACTLY two converted rules.

qa_findings_addressed.qa_finding: Genuinely safe Class-1 candidates are the verb-WORD-anchored subset; the AC could not distinguish a safe conversion from an unsafe one.
qa_findings_addressed.resolution: FIXED. AC-A1 now names the EXACTLY two convertible rules and asserts that NO git rule, NO docker/systemctl rule, and NO path/value rule reads COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED. Additionally, QA pass1's example list of 'genuinely safe' docker/systemctl rules was itself over-inclusive by its own stated criterion: docker compose down|restart|stop, docker ... happy, docker system prune -a, systemctl ... docker all have their verb/subcommand/target in a quotable ARGUMENT position of the non-DANGER command words docker/systemctl (docker compose "down" is a real destructive down but strips to 'docker compose '). They are therefore moved to DO-NOT-CONVERT. The only command-word-anchored rule is dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred.

qa_findings_addressed.qa_finding: AC-A3 used echo "git reset --hard" as the canonical should-not-block example, which is precisely the case that PROVES the false negative for a converted reset rule.
qa_findings_addressed.resolution: FIXED. AC-A2/AC-A3 now use rule-specific payloads for the two actually-converted rules: dd (block: 'dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda'; no-block: 'echo "dd ..."') and kill (block: 'kill 1234' AND 'kill "1234"'; no-block: 'echo "kill 1234"'). A NOTE explicitly states echo "git reset --hard" is NOT used because the git reset rule is not converted (it stays raw and correctly continues to match its quoted-string mention as pre-existing behavior).

qa_findings_addressed.qa_finding: AC-A1 was not safely measurable (textual COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED reference would pass for an unsafe conversion that silently disables a block).
qa_findings_addressed.resolution: FIXED. AC-A1 now states it is satisfied ONLY when AC-A2 AND AC-A3 both pass for both converted rules with rule-specific payloads; the textual reference alone is declared insufficient evidence.

convertible_safe_to_strip.why_safe: command-word-anchored; the command word token is preserved verbatim in the stripped view, so match is identical raw-vs-stripped; conversion removes false positives like echo "dd if=..." with zero false-negative risk

argument-anchored (kill\s+[0-9]) BUT kill is already in DANGER_COMMANDS so the stripper exposes its args (kill "1234" -> kill 1234); match identical raw-vs-stripped; mirrors the already-stripped kill -sig rule at :1119

do_not_convert_argument_anchored_git.rule: git update-ref / branch -D/-d/--delete/--force/--move / symbolic-ref ... HEAD refs/
git stash push/save/create/store/-u/--all + bare git stash

net_scope_delta_vs_pass1: pass1 estimated ~12 convertible 'verb' rules + DANGER_COMMANDS additions. pass2 converts EXACTLY 2 rules (dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred :1045, kill <PID> :1311) and adds ZERO DANGER_COMMANDS entries. The git and docker/systemctl rules move from convertible to forbidden. Item A's blast radius shrinks substantially; the high-risk false-negative surface is eliminated.

evidence_basis.reference_tier: tier_2_verified (all cited file:line read directly this session)

items_unchanged.F: approved as-is (commit.md:221 stale label); no change
items_unchanged.C: approved as-is (verify-only, 3-site marker parity, patch-on-gap); no change
items_unchanged.B: approved as-is (verify-only, candidate-key parity + CF-1 overwrite-only invariant, patch-on-gap); no change
